title: Inspect & Measure: dimension/measure tool, edge-map smart snapping, alignment guides
goal: The user's PRIMARY workflow: screenshotting UX and redlining it — measuring gaps/sizes, checking alignment, calling out spacing inconsistencies. Build a designer's redline kit on the existing non-destructive annotation model: a measure/dimension tool whose pixel sizes are auto-labeled (toggleable, restyleable, per-layer), plus SMART SNAPPING that magnetizes measure endpoints and alignment guides to UI-element edges detected in the screenshot bitmap (there is NO UI tree — snapping comes from image edge analysis). Auto-inconsistency detection is a timeboxed spike after the core lands. Sequenced AHEAD of phases 14/15 because it is the main use case.

decisions.units: REVISED 2026-06-28 (user): readouts default to PIXELS (raw image px), with a per-measure pt|px toggle (pixels FIRST in the control). `points` divides raw px by `PhotonzDocument.pixelScale` (default 1; ≤0→1 guard). Document coords == image pixels (`canvasSize = ref.pixelSize`, Document.swift:23). The 1×/2× image-scale inspector control was REMOVED as confusing; `pixelScale` plumbing stays for a future AUTO-DETECT from the capture's DPI/backingScaleFactor (NOT yet wired — so points==pixels at the default scale of 1 until auto-detect lands). Stroke width is in LOGICAL pixels (×pixelScale at render) so a 1px sizer line aligns to the pixel grid.
decisions.snapping: REVISED through user testing (final 2026-07-02; the original global-projection design FAILED — text baselines are local and dilute to nothing across a full-width image). Final: EdgeMap stores block-summed DIRECTIONAL Sobel fields (|Gy|→horizontal boundaries, |Gx|→vertical) + perceptual luma; queries are WINDOWED by the moving ruler line's span. Acceptance = absolute floor 0.12 only (window-relative thresholds drown faint hairlines next to strong edges). Per-side luma LANDINGS put the snap on the first visually-clean background row (AA glow = element, sparse descenders = background → baseline semantics). Strength-weighted pick, text-run approach-side filtering, motion-direction axis gating, ⌘ bypass, tolerance max(8pt/zoom, 4px), pixel-grid fallback. Full rationale in docs/design/tools.md § Edge snapping. The existing `Snapping` enum (layer frame → canvas edges) remains separate.
decisions.model: Measure is its own LayerContent.measure on the two-endpoint pattern (start/end = box corners). `MeasureForm` {line, bracket}; tool DEFAULTS to BRACKET (a squared 'U' wrapping a gap: drag corner A->opposite B, legs reach in from A, connector spans the gap on the far side, label OUTSIDE). bracketAxis = dominant box axis (tall->vertical, wide->horizontal); inspector has explicit Direction toggle + invert (swap corners). The numeric LABEL is part of the layer (toggleable/restyleable). Select shows a dotted box + handles on ALL 4 box corners (line shows 2 ends); corner drag updates the right start/end components (diagonal-opposite fixed), live label, one undo step. Alignment guides (16.6) are a separate persistent-line layer.
decisions.sequencing: 16.1->16.2->16.3 deliver the measure tool (the anchor). 16.4->16.5 deliver the edge map + smart snapping (the killer combo with measure). 16.6 adds alignment guides. 16.7 is the auto-inspect SPIKE, only after 16.1-16.6 prove the edge map is reliable, with a simpler cross-highlight fallback if full auto-detection is flaky.

tasks.title: Measurement model + dimension geometry (core, TDD)
tasks.notes: DONE (490 tests green, +19). New `Sources/PhotonzCore/Measure.swift`: MeasureMode {free|horizontal|vertical}, MeasureUnit {points|pixels}, MeasureCapStyle {ticks|arrows}, MeasureSegment, MeasureGeometry, MeasureContent (start/end + strokeWidth/colorHex/showLabel/unit/decimals/capStyle), MeasureBuilder (layer/updating/resized/restyled, mirrors AnnotationBuilder). Distance: free=hypot, H/V=abs(dx/dy); displayDistance divides by pixelScale for points (0->1 guard), raw for pixels; label() = '%.<decimals>f <suffix>'. Geometry: free=bare segment; H/V level the dimension line onto the END point's axis and drop a witness line from an off-axis START. NEW LayerContent.measure case threaded through: Layer.measure accessor + measureEndpoint + contains (hit near dim+witness lines) + hasEndpointHandles; AnnotationEditing.allowsFrameResize/resized routing; Document.pixelScale (default 1, custom Decodable for legacy back-compat). DocumentRenderer has a NO-OP .measure stub (returns nil) until 16.2. Tests: MeasureTests.swift (distance/units/geometry/builder/pixelScale round-trip+legacy). NOTE: H/V level onto END point's axis (drag-end = where the dimension line sits) — 16.3 drag UX should honor that.

tasks.title: Dimension rasterizer (render, pixel-tested)
tasks.notes: DONE (496 tests green, +6 render). New Sources/PhotonzRender/MeasureRasterizer.swift draws in layer-local top-left space: witness/extension lines, the dimension line, end caps (ticks = perpendicular serif; arrows = inward filled arrowheads), and a toggleable numeric label on a rounded plate filled with the measure color + white CoreText text (AppKit-free CTFramesetter/CTFrameDraw, mirrors TextRasterizer's flipped-context pattern). DocumentRenderer .measure case now rasterizes (replaced the 16.1 no-op stub) with variant 'scale:<pixelScale>' so the points readout invalidates the cache when the document scale changes. GOTCHA fixed: MeasureBuilder.layer now adopts the real span (content.start/end = from/to) BEFORE computing renderPadding/estimatedLabelSize, else updating()/short measures reserved the wrong label width. Frame reserves estimatedLabelSize (centered on the dimension line) so short labels aren't clipped. Tests: MeasureRenderingTests (line, plate on/off, witness, diagonal, Retina pixelScale) + core label-reservation/frame tests. FOLLOW-UP for 16.3: verify label text orientation visually (pixel test only checks presence).

tasks.title: Measure tool UX (app) — INTERACTIVELY VERIFIED, grew well beyond original scope
tasks.notes: DONE + user-verified (502 tests green). Tool.measure ('ruler', M). Base: CanvasView measureDrag/preview, EditorState.addMeasure, MeasureInspector (InspectorSectionID.measure), EditorView wiring. THEN iterated heavily with the user (commits af34f1c..328bf04): (1) BRACKET FORM is now the default — drag-a-box 'U' wrapping a gap, label outside; MeasureForm{line,bracket}, bracketGeometry/bracketAxis/labelCenter; ⇧ flips axis on create; preview draws the bracket path. (2) SELECT/MOVE/RESIZE: dotted selection box + handles on ALL 4 box corners (MeasureCornerDrag(xFromEnd,yFromEnd); measureCorners()); live label via previewMeasureEndpoints, one undo via commitMeasureEndpoints; move via Layer.contains hitting the drawn strokes; editEndpoint unifies annotation+measure endpoint hit-testing. (3) Inspector: Style(Bracket/Line), Direction(V/H) + invert ⇄ (invertMeasure swaps corners), Unit(Pixels FIRST/Points), Thickness(1/2/3px), Show-label, Color; image-scale control REMOVED. (4) Stroke width = LOGICAL px (×pixelScale, butt caps+miter joins) default 1; unit default PIXELS. (5) Label orientation bug FIXED (blit TextRasterizer glyphs, don't draw CoreText into the flipped ctx) + orientation guard test. (6) Universal selection outline (systemBlue, 2px, dotted, 60%) for ALL object types, HIDDEN during any resize. (7) SEPARATE BUG FIXED + verified: region capture overlay no longer activates the app / raises the editor (non-activating panels, see capture.md). REMAINING FOLLOW-UPS: measureStyle not persisted; pixelScale auto-detect from capture DPI not wired; toolbar S popover doesn't cover measures.

tasks.title: Edge-map analysis (render + core, TDD)
tasks.notes: REVISED 2026-06-28: switched from CIEdges (combined magnitude) to DIRECTIONAL Sobel after the user reported text baselines/top-lines snapping poorly. Reproduced on a real dark-mode capture (1313×427): combined magnitude smeared a glyph row into a thicket of weak horizontal edges (max strength ~0.29, baseline indistinguishable) because vertical stems' |Gx| inflates every row of the band. FIX: CIColorMatrix luma → CIConvolution3X3 Sobel Gx & Gy → render each to a single-channel FLOAT buffer (.Rf — 8-bit would clamp the signed half, halving edges) → project |Gx|→X (vertical boundaries), |Gy|→Y (horizontal). Now the same capture's baseline is a clean dominant peak (strength 0.81, whitespace below drops to 0.06); dividers/dock still 1.0/0.9. Default threshold lowered 0.25→0.2 so the x-height top line (~0.22) also clears (text tops are inherently spread for italic/ascender glyphs — baseline is the reliable target). Added render test detectsTextCapLineAndBaselineNotTheBandInterior (asserts NO interior-band edge). ORIGINAL: DONE (516 tests green, +14). CORE `Sources/PhotonzCore/EdgeMap.swift`: EdgeCandidate{position,strength}, EdgeMap{width,height,verticalEdges(x cols),horizontalEdges(y rows)} (Equatable/Sendable/Codable, .empty, top-left space, sorted asc), EdgeMap.from(xProfile,yProfile,...) assembler, and EdgeProfile.peaks(profile,threshold=0.25,minSeparation=4): local-maxima above (threshold × max) → non-max suppression (strongest first, tie→lower position) within minSeparation, strength normalized to peak=1.0. RENDER `Sources/PhotonzRender/EdgeMapAnalyzer.swift`: CIEdges → crop to extent → render(toBitmap:RGBA8) → project R channel onto X (columns) and Y (rows); render(toBitmap:) is CI bottom-left so yProfile is REVERSED into top-left order before peak-finding. Shared CIContext (one pass per image). EdgeMapCache (NSLock, keyed by ImageRef.id, +invalidate) satisfies 'cache per ImageRef' — 16.5 wires it into the app. TESTS: EdgeMapTests (empty/flat→none, white-rect→L/R peaks, sort, noise<threshold rejected, NMS dedup keeps stronger, beyond-sep both kept, strength normalize, Codable round-trip); EdgeMapAnalyzerTests (real CIEdges: 100×80 white rect → verticalEdges≈20/80, horizontalEdges≈15/65 within ±3px; flat→none; cache identity). GOTCHA recorded: projection onto X is flip-invariant; only Y needs the reversal. FOLLOW-UP for 16.5: EdgeSnapping consumes EdgeMap + adds pixel-grid snap; app holds one EdgeMapCache (likely on AppCoordinator/EditorState) and calls edgeMap(for:store:) lazily on first measure drag.

tasks.title: Ruler snapping: corner grips magnetize to detected edges + pixel grid (core + app) — USER'S NEXT ASK
tasks.notes: DONE after several user-feedback rounds (533 tests green); user confirms it feels right. FINAL DESIGN (differs from the original global-projection plan — see log 2026-07-01/02): EdgeMap stores block-summed (16px) directional |Gx|/|Gy| fields + PERCEPTUAL luma; queries are WINDOWED (horizontalEdges(inXRange:)/verticalEdges(inYRange:)) — the moving ruler line's span windows the candidates, so a leg snaps to edges it actually crosses (text baselines are local!). Acceptance = ABSOLUTE floor 0.12 only (a window-relative threshold was tried and REMOVED: it drowned faint hairline borders sharing a window with a strong panel edge). Each EdgeCandidate carries edgeBefore/edgeAfter luma LANDINGS = first visually-clean bg row on each side (residual ≤10% of edge contrast; AA glow excluded, sparse descender ink included as bg — user measures from the BASELINE); EdgeSnapping snaps the pointer-side landing, strength-weighted (score=strength/(1+d/4)), with text-run approach-side filtering (clusters ≥3 lines within 40px: pointer below run midpoint only sees baseline/box-bottom), tolerance max(8pt/zoom, 4px), pixel-grid fallback. App: snapping in CREATE drag (anchor + growing corner) AND corner-resize; ⌘ bypass; motion-direction axis gating (decisive vertical motion suppresses X captures — no perpendicular guide bars); guides reuse snapGuideLayer. EditorState computes the map OFF-MAIN (Task.detached; ~2s debug on 7MP), gated to measure tool/selection, cached per ImageRef. GOTCHAS: render(toBitmap:) rows are TOP-first (a 'flip' relocated edges — caught only by an ASYMMETRIC fixture); changing EdgeCandidate's layout with stale incremental objects segfaulted the test runner (clean rebuild cures). BONUS this session (user request): freeze-frame ⌘⇧4 region capture (RectSelection.swift) — screenshot-first, shielding-level frozen overlay, crosshair via key non-activating panel, crop from frozen bitmap, animationBehavior=.none; user: 'friggin perfect'. ORIGINAL NOTES: CORE DONE + WIRED, awaiting user verify (522 tests green, +6). `Sources/PhotonzCore/EdgeSnapping.swift`: EdgeSnapping.snap(point, edges:EdgeMap, zoom, screenTolerance=6, snapToPixelGrid=true) → Snap{point, guideX?, guideY?}. Per-axis independent: nearest in-tolerance vertical edge captures x (else round to pixel grid), nearest horizontal captures y; tolerance = screenTolerance/zoom (constant feel). Captured edge returned as guideX/Y for the highlight; grid/free snaps return nil guide. Tests EdgeSnappingTests: snaps within tol, no-op beyond tol, nearest-of-two, pixel-grid fallback, tolerance scales w/ zoom, axes independent. APP WIRING: Layer.imageRef accessor (core); EditorState holds one EdgeMapCache + `measureEdgeMap` computed var (GATED: only computes the GPU/CPU edge sweep when measure tool active OR a measure is selected, else .empty; cached after first use, survives undo). EditorView passes editorState.measureEdgeMap → CanvasView.edgeMap → CanvasNSView. In mouseDragged's measureCornerDrag branch the dragged corner is snapped via EdgeSnapping before previewMeasureEndpoints; ⌘ held = free drag (bypass snap). snapGuide state drives a full-span highlight reusing the existing snapGuideLayer (shared w/ move-drag guides); cleared on mouse-up/Esc/tool-switch. REMAINING/VERIFY: confirm edges actually detected on a real UX screenshot (CIEdges threshold may need tuning per content); consider snapping the NEW-measure drag end too (currently only corner-resize snaps); highlight color currently = move-snap guide style. The user's 'rough-in a bracket then drag a corner onto the real UI edge' flow. UPDATE 2026-07-01: analyzer verified GOOD on the user's real 3456×2234 Google capture (56 H-edges incl. the menu divider as the STRONGEST at strength 1.0; 46 V-edges) — so snap candidates definitely exist and coords align (drag point + edgeMap both in image-pixel space). BUT user reports corners still don't grab obvious lines. OPEN: instrument the live drag (env-guarded NSLog was added then removed) to confirm whether edgeMap reaches the view at drag time (gating/SwiftUI timing) vs the 6-screen-pt magnet being too tight at high zoom. Likely fix: widen tolerance and/or also snap the CREATE drag (currently only corner-resize snaps, not initial draw). ALSO shipped this session (16.3 follow-up polish): bracket connector/label moved to the START side (label above for horizontal, left for vertical — matches user redlines); removed size-dependent axis auto-detect (bracketAxis deleted) — brackets now default to VERTICAL via persisted measureStyle.mode and never flip based on drag proportions; MeasureInspector restyled to match Effects panel (caption-above-control, Direction lists Horizontal first, color label-left).
tasks.notesOld: This is what the user calls 'ruler snapping' (their 3rd measure request). New EdgeSnapping (core): given a dragged point, the EdgeMap, zoom, and a screen tolerance (~6pt), snap X to the nearest in-tolerance vertical edge and Y to the nearest horizontal edge; ALSO snap to the integer PIXEL GRID (round to image px) so thin 1px sizer lines land crisp (the user explicitly wants pixel-perfect alignment — see the 16.3 logical-px stroke work). Return snapped point + which guide(s) captured (for a highlight overlay). App: wire into the MeasureCornerDrag in CanvasView (mouseDragged computes the dragged corner, snaps it before previewMeasureEndpoints), render a highlight on the snapped edge while held. The user's workflow: rough-in a bracket, then drag the misaligned top/bottom (or any) corner and have it click onto the real UI edge. TESTS FIRST: snaps to nearest peak within tolerance, no-op beyond tolerance, picks nearest of two, snaps to pixel grid, tolerance scales with zoom. NOTE: snapping applies per-axis independently (a corner can snap X to an edge while Y free).

tasks.title: Alignment guides: draggable snapping rulers (core + app)
tasks.notes: Persistent H/V guide lines dropped on the canvas, draggable, snapping to edges via 16.5; toggleable + configurable (color, on/off) as layers. Serves 'draw lines on the edges of text to check alignment.' Core: guide model + axis position clamp to canvas. App: drop/drag/delete, ruler affordance, render. Mostly app; keep position/clamp math in core with a couple of tests.

tasks.title: Auto-inspect spike: flag spacing inconsistencies (after 16.1-16.6)
tasks.notes: TIMEBOXED EXPLORATION, only once the edge map proves reliable. From the EdgeMap, find the dominant bands/columns, measure gaps between consecutive edges, take the mode, and FLAG outliers (16,16,16,17 -> highlight the 17 + delta). Scope via marquee. If full auto-detection is flaky, ship the simpler fallback: a row of gap-measures that cross-highlight mismatched values. Explicitly research-y; capture findings in notes regardless of how far it gets.
